title: Shri Shiv ji ki aarti
intro: Read Shiv Ji Ki Aarti in Hindi and seek the divine blessings of Lord Shiva.
description:
Om Jai Shiv Omkara,
Swami Jai Shiv Omkara.
Brahma, Vishnu, Sadashiva,
Ardhangi dhara॥
॥ Om Jai Shiv Omkara ॥

Ekanan, Chaturanan,
Panchanan raje.
Hansasan, Garudasan,
Vrishvahan saje॥
॥ Om Jai Shiv Omkara ॥

Do bhuj, char chaturbhuj,
Dasbhuj ati sohe.
Trigun roop nirakhta,
Tribhuvan jan mohe॥
॥ Om Jai Shiv Omkara ॥

Akshamala, vanmala,
Mundmala dhari.
Tripurari, Kansari,
Kar mala dhari॥
॥ Om Jai Shiv Omkara ॥

Kar ke madhya kamandalu,
Chakra trishuldhari.
Sukhkari, dukhahari,
Jagpalan kari॥
॥ Om Jai Shiv Omkara ॥

Brahma, Vishnu, Sadashiva,
Janat aviveka.
Pranavakshar ke madhye,
Ye teenon eka॥
॥ Om Jai Shiv Omkara ॥

Kashi mein Vishwanath,
Virajat Nandi Brahmachari.
Nit uthi bhog lagawat,
Mahima ati bhari॥
॥ Om Jai Shiv Omkara ॥

Trigun Shivji ki aarti,
Jo koi nar gaave.
Kahat Shivanand Swami,
Manvanchit phal paave॥
॥ Om Jai Shiv Omkara ॥

faq.q1: What is the main purpose and benefit of performing Shiv Aarti?
faq.a1: The main purpose of performing Shiv Aarti is to express gratitude towards Lord Shiva. It brings peace to the mind, promotes spiritual energy, and helps remove negativity through the blessings of Lord Shiva.
faq.q2: What is the best time to perform Shiv Aarti?
faq.a2: Morning and evening (Sandhya Kaal) are considered the most auspicious times for Shiv Aarti. Performing it on Mondays, either in temples or at home, is considered highly fruitful.
faq.q3: Can we perform Shiv Aarti at home?
faq.a3: Yes, you can perform Shiv Aarti at home with full devotion and purity. You can light a lamp and incense sticks in front of a Shivling or an idol/picture of Lord Shiva.
faq.a4: Yes, women can perform Shiv Aarti with full devotion. There are no gender-based restrictions on worshipping Lord Shiva or performing his Aarti.
faq.q5: What should be done after performing Aarti?
faq.a5: After completing the Aarti, one should circle their hands around the flame (diya) and offer a saashtang pranam (full prostration) to the deity. It is also considered auspicious to chant the mantra 'Karpur Gauram Karunavtaram' afterward.
